vibe: Fresh finds for Friday the 13th. Deep house, Japanese indie, Latin grooves, soul.

tracks.name: Devoted To U
tracks.reason: Folamour's signature feel-good deep house — warm, soulful, and impossibly groovy. If you like Jimpster, this is the French equivalent.

tracks.name: The Break Up
tracks.reason: Amsterdam deep house duo Dam Swindle at their smoothest. Jazzy keys over a locked-in groove — proper headnod music.

tracks.name: Midnight in Peckham
tracks.artist: Chaos In The CBD, Isaac Aesili
tracks.reason: New Zealand's Chaos In The CBD channelling late-night London energy. Deep, dubby, hypnotic — Isaac Aesili's sax takes it somewhere else entirely.

tracks.name: Rare Happiness
tracks.reason: Hunee is one of the most respected selectors in dance music. This track is warm, strange, and beautiful — like finding a rare record in a Tokyo basement shop.

tracks.name: YUKON
tracks.reason: Unexpected pull from Bieber's catalogue — stripped-back, melancholic, almost lo-fi. Different energy from what you'd expect.

tracks.name: One (feat. tofubeats)
tracks.reason: STUTS x tofubeats is a Tokyo dream collab. Boom-bap meets Shibuya-kei electronics — the kind of track that makes a Nakameguro walk feel cinematic.

tracks.name: Magic!
tracks.reason: Kan Sano is a Tokyo keys wizard. Jazz-trained, lo-fi sensibility, Rhodes for days. This one floats.

tracks.name: I need your kiss (sadagain version)
tracks.reason: Dreamy, washed-out Japanese bedroom pop. The kind of song that sounds like it's coming through a window on a warm night.

tracks.name: Cakes
tracks.reason: Homecomings are Kyoto indie pop perfection. Jangly guitars, sweet vocals, and that specific Japanese indie melancholy that hits different.

tracks.name: Barracuda
tracks.reason: Miho Hatori (ex-Cibo Matto) goes experimental. Art-pop with teeth — weird, wonderful, and utterly original.

tracks.name: Vámono
tracks.reason: Puerto Rican duo Buscabulla blend synth-pop with Latin rhythms. Dark, danceable, and completely addictive.

tracks.name: EMERGENCIA - Presented by PlayStation, Horizon Forbidden West
tracks.album: EMERGENCIA (Presented by PlayStation, Horizon Forbidden West)
tracks.reason: Nathy Peluso is Argentine chaos energy in the best way — flamenco, hip-hop, soul, all smashed together. This collab goes hard.

tracks.name: Me Voy
tracks.reason: Cuban funk meets Havana street party. Cimafunk is Afro-Cuban funk revival — think if James Brown grew up in Cuba.

tracks.name: Dream in Colour
tracks.reason: Franc Moody are London disco-funk that sounds like it belongs in a Daikanyama boutique. Shimmery, feel-good, impossible not to move to.

tracks.name: Anomalie bleue
tracks.reason: L'Impératrice are French synth-pop royalty. Lush, cinematic, very Daft Punk's cooler older sister energy.

tracks.name: Lightenup
tracks.reason: Parcels are Australian disco-funk via Berlin. Tight harmonies, crisp production — like if Bee Gees went to Berghain.

tracks.name: Socially Awkward
tracks.reason: Kiefer is an LA jazz pianist who makes beats. Warm Rhodes chords, head-nodding drums — perfect study/work music.

tracks.name: Tadow
tracks.reason: Masego's breakout — a live looping saxophone jam that builds into something hypnotic. Jazz, R&B, and trap all at once.

tracks.name: Fine Wine
tracks.reason: Saint Motel bring indie pop with a wink. Catchy without being obvious, polished without being sterile.

tracks.name: Glass
tracks.reason: Hania Rani is a Polish pianist who makes music that sounds like light moving through water. Minimal, beautiful, meditative.
